Content: Massive cultivation tanks house many ancient plant species you've never seen on the surface. Life was once scarce in these underground caverns, until the Reactor Drive ignited here. In its relentless pursuit of progress, the Spacetrek Collective did not forget to retrieve the dormant seeds that slept beneath the Roya Frostlands.

Academy Event Notice

Hi there! We've been waiting for you!
The New Solar Celebration is about to begin. Come on down to the event venue and soak up the incredible energy!

But that's not all... Have you heard the rumors? Someone's discovered the Academy's "Dark Side"! We're talking mysterious graffiti, strange, twisting hallways that stretch on forever, and secrets that are usually kept hidden... 

We bet you're just as curious as we are to explore this Dark Side! What kind of riddles and games await this time?

We heard an especially special riddle was hidden away somewhere. Solve it, and some serious luck may just come your way!
Sigrika?
Senior?
Yes, I'm here.
I failed… I couldn't do it… I'm sorry, I'm so, so sorry…
It's not your fault, Sigrika. What did you see in the Dark Side?
I don't know. It's all wrong... Why... Why is this happening...?
It felt like a nightmare... No, not a nightmare. It was all real.
It's all my fault. The Tacet Discords, the riddles… all the disturbances in the Dark Side are coming from me.
I'm not brave enough to face failure, so I tell myself I can handle everything. I'm terrified of letting everyone down… of not living up to expectations.
I'm so scared. What do I do now…?
(Comfort her.)
(Let her calm down.)
It was just a nightmare. We got out with those students, remember? You did everything you could, and you did well.
Was it… really just a nightmare?
That voice, the one telling us to stay… I know who it is now.
It's me...
I was the one who made it. That's why I could solve the riddles and see those Tacet Discords. It told me... I would always end up back there.
What should I do to stop it from getting worse?
There's no "right" answer I can give you.
But whatever you decide to do, I'll be there to help you.
...But isn't that just relying on someone else?
No. The fight is yours to finish. But you won't be fighting it alone.
You once told me that you hear so many voices when you close your eyes.
The things you heard in the Dark Side aren't so different from what I'm telling you now. They're all just... voices.
What matters isn't how they make you feel, but what you choose to do after hearing them.
Even if what I choose ends up making things worse?
Even then, it'll be okay. At least we've tried our best till the end and have no regrets.
I know someone who saw her own end coming from a mile away. Someone very dear to me. But she walked right toward it, towards her own sacrifice.
She wanted to save the world. In the end, she kept her promise.
And I... I'm far from perfect. There are things I can't do, either.
But no matter what happens, I won't regret the choices I've made.
So I should try? Even if I might fail?
Senior, I... need some time to think about this.
What she did... I couldn't judge. Still, I saw her resolve and courage. Those could never be wrong.

When Sigrika wakes up in the real world, you're there to comfort her. 
You tell her that even if you can't give her a "right" answer, you'll help her face whatever comes next. 
This isn't about getting it right or wrong. It's about going all the way, so neither of you has any regrets.
So I should try? Even if I might fail?
Senior, I... need some time to think about this.
I have two tickets to a movie. Can I tempt you with a diversion?
From the title, it's an old road trip film from the last century.
Come find me in the screening room if you're interested.
Right on time. The movie is about to start.
Come on, {PlayerName}. Let's go inside.
Hm… looks like the protagonist seems a little lost.
Remind you of Sigrika at all?
It does. I actually spoke with her after the incident.
She asked what I would do if I were in her place.
I told her I'd do everything within my power not to leave behind any regrets.
But I can say that from the comfort of my experience.
Has she reached out to you since then, {PlayerName}?
Not yet.
I'm not sure how she's doing now.
It must have hit her hard.
Perhaps you should go see her.
She's spent so long trying to live up to others' expectations, she's forgotten she needs to face her own first.
And that takes a different kind of courage.
Especially when "she" is the very "<te href=800035>eye</te>" of the Dark Side's vortex.
So it's confirmed?
Yes. Mornye confirmed that the abnormal frequency matches the Tacet Discord you encountered. It's the source.
Now we can finally drag that shadow into the light.
Precisely. It told Sigrika she would always return. To make that happen, it will have to issue a new riddle.
As for the rest, that's up to Sigrika now.
…Look. Even this movie's protagonist is choosing to go back to where it all began.
After everything she's been through, she still chooses to face what she once ran from.
A fitting ending, don't you think?

Lucilla invites you to watch a movie with her. 
She speaks again of Sigrika's situation and confirms that the Reflections and Tacet Discord you ran into back in the Dark Side are the real source of the incident.
A good film, wasn't it?
It was interesting.
Sorry, I wasn't paying much attention...
I'm glad.
A shame I don't have a spare ticket for a second viewing.
Next time, we can try something from a different genre.
{PlayerName}, the Divinator released a new riddle just now.
I'll be counting on you to make this the last one.
A new riddle's out… I should tell Sigrika.
A new riddle...
Sigrika, the Divinator just released another riddle.
I'm going to go check it out.
I'm going to go check it out.
(No reply from her... guess I'll go see the Divinator myself first.)
Senior.
You've come.
Mm-hmm.
I've been talking to a lot of people lately.
Lynae told me about her past and how she felt lost before she came to Startorch.
But she's happy with her life here now.
Professor Lucilla said that no matter what happens, she believes I can face it. I'm not alone in this. With you, with her, with Dr. Luuk… none of us will let the Dark Side ruin the Academy.
And Denia... She just told me to drop it all and make life easier for myself. That's just her way of caring.
And you, Senior... I remember every word you said to me.
All your voices are like gold... I've been holding them close, thinking.
If I'm the one who made the Dark Side like this, then going back and solving riddles is exactly what the Fractsidus wants, right?
But if I stop now, will the "eye" of the vortex become someone else, and trap them like it trapped me in the Dark Side?
Each of these riddles... is a reflection of my own fears and doubts. Do I run away from it all, or do I go face "myself" in the Dark Side?
I still don't know the answer. But... here I am. Confusing, isn't it?
I'm glad you're here.
You've already taken an important step.
I want to find the answer myself.
Then let's see what the riddle says.
Didi, please show me the new riddle.
Dididi... to solve the riddle, first—
No, Didi. Just give it to me.
There's only one line on it...
"Go now. To where the revelry began, and face your true heart."
The revelry...
It must mean the New Solar Celebration.
Then it's beginning... must be the entrance to the celebration venue?
Let's go check it out.

You visit the Divinator together with Sigrika to decipher the final riddle. 
After sitting with it for a few days, Sigrika finally shares what she thinks the answer could be.
You decide to confront the vortex in the Dark Side together.
Look, the entrance rune is there.
Feeling nervous?
A little.
This last riddle says I'll have to face my true heart.
But… what will it look like? My failures? The cost of running away? Or maybe…
I don't know. So…
You're not alone.
Look at me, Senior.
If it gets to that point, make sure to wake me up, okay?
Alright, time to go. In three… two…
One...
Say cheese!
You find the entrance rune. Sigrika takes a breath, steadies herself, and together you step back into the Dark Side.
Senior!
Are you alright?
How do you feel now?
Thank you, Senior.
When I close my eyes now, I still hear everyone's hopes for me. But I hear something else, too. Their laughter and cheers.
It's like gold flecked with starlight. It tells me...
...That everyone loved the celebration.
I think that's the answer to the final riddle.
I'd say this calls for the special candy.
Are these... Rainbow Bean Pop Rocks?
With an improved formula. Not on the market yet.
So the one who makes these is—?
Shhh... Our little secret.
Again with the candy, Dr. Luuk? I'll be confiscating the extras.
Well done, both of you. The vortex in the Dark Side is almost gone, and we've already reclaimed our access control from that Tacet Discord.
(Not completely...?)
For now, just enjoy the celebration.
Sigrika! Over here! Chisa's getting a new top record!
Coming! You need to try this, Lynae—it's the new Rainbow Bean Pop Rocks!
{PlayerName}.
The Grand Architect's game isn't over yet.
Yes. There are still more shadows to deal with.
Senior, we're over here!
Go now.
There's a white bird here! I've spotted it several times, but there's no record of it. It's definitely a new species!
Hey, Senior... look over here.
In three... two... one...
Say cheese!
You resolve the issue of the vortex and leave the Academy's Dark Side behind. There might still be a few ripples left, but the real danger is over. For now, at least, you can enjoy a quiet night.
Almost done over here! Good thing you two came to help.
Easy-peasy! If anything comes up, just gimme a shout.
And me.
What are you all doing?
{PlayerName}! Perfect timing! Here, take this.
What's this?
It's a souvenir ticket for the New Solar Celebration.

Means the celebration is officially over. We didn't have the "Minerva's Treasure" wishing ceremony this year, so the Student Council made these instead. Isla wanted everyone to have something to remember the event by.
Denia was supposed to hand them out, but she's taken a few days off. Odd that Nivora's on leave, too. I wonder if they planned it...
There's so much to do, even with these simple tasks. I'm just glad to have your help.
It's nothing. I actually came here hoping to find a location to rebuild that polymorphic thermocyclic composite roasting device.
Too bad there isn't one. If there's ever another event like this, I'll make a wish to test some new devices in the Academy.
A wish, huh… I've got one too!
You have a new wish?
Is it to capture a certain bird's face?
My wish is—
Heh, I'm keeping it a secret for now! You'll find out what it is once it comes true.
Since when did you learn to tease people like that, Sigrika?
Consider it a riddle! Some wishes are better kept to yourself... don't you think?
I'd rather find out right now. Chisa, get her!
On it!
Hey! No cheating!
Surrounded by laughter and bright young smiles, this fleeting celebration comes to a gentle close, along with this chapter of your journey.
I still think we should let her in on our plan. You know how desperately that girl craves validation.
We can. But not while she's like this.
You once said she lacks confidence, Dr. Luuk.
From where I'm standing, she has too much of it.
I see that she's a fighter. She believes she's capable of living up to everyone's expectations.
Or rather, she thinks she must. She may as well be walking a tightrope.
I've seen many students like her.
Every single one believed they could always be the best. That failure wasn't an option. So they pushed, and pushed...
Remember that student I mentioned, {PlayerName}? The one with the flawed but brilliant hypotheses?
She didn't just leave Startorch Academy.
The Academy... lost her for good.
She lost her balance on that wire.
That is exactly what the Fractsidus wants to dig their claws into.
Here you are, Siggy. Where's that new friend of yours? Let me guess... they're off planning something big, but didn't want you in on it?
I just wasn't good enough.
Here. Dusk Honeypot flavor. I know it's your favorite.
What if we just dropped the whole riddle thing? Or... is it about proving worthy of being a future Heliodic Six successor?
I just... don't want to let anyone down.
Every single student came to the Academy with a heart full of promise.
That promise is what keeps this Academy standing.
We will not sit back and watch the Fractsidus exploit that. And let's not forget, the knowledge these students hold can shape the future of Lahai-Roi itself.
Which means we're in a race against time. That Tacet Discord was feeding on energy. If the Dark Side is a vortex of emotion, then that thing is the eye of the storm.
We have to find it.
You may want to stock up on the candy, Dr. Luuk.
Can I apply for reimbursement?
Anyway, {PlayerName}, just get some rest. We'll keep an eye on the Dark Side.
Don't worry. This game won't end the way the Fractsidus intends.
After hearing Lucilla out, you decide to keep investigating the Dark Side. Whatever game the Fractsidus thinks they're playing, they're not going to win.
You remember the place Lucilla mentioned. It should be in the dormitory where you once went looking for Lynae.
Is that a new entrance to the Dark Side?
Why is it popping up in the classroom again? What's the riddle behind it this time?
Is that President Lucilla? What happened?
Heard someone went missing. Ha... I really hope everything's alright.
Runes and shadows... The picture from the riddle comes to mind as you glance at a certain spot ahead.
For now, things have settled. While you wait for the next riddle, you've got some "free time" to yourself.
Sigrika looks like something's on her mind. Maybe you should check on her.
The girl lowers her gaze by the bedside, crying quietly. Maybe she needs some time alone with her thoughts.
The invitation is oddly timed, but you decide to head to the meeting spot anyway.
Things have finally begun to settle. Time to actually enjoy the celebration while you can.
She was right there in front of me, and then she just... Ngh...
Don't worry, the professors will sort this out.
This has never happened before. I've got a bad feeling about this.
Like a gut feeling?! Well, let's just steer clear of riddles for now then...
Where did Denia go? I need her help for something.
She's probably off napping somewhere.
Lynae's fingers fly across the controller, racking up an impossibly high score. She's completely immersed in the game, seemingly oblivious to your presence...
{PlayerName}, there's a matter regarding the New Solar Celebration I'd like to discuss in detail.
If you have a moment, come find me at these coordinates.
And keep this in mind: When the one from afar touches the "truth," the door of dreams will appear.
How do you feel, {PlayerName}?
I'm fine.
My head hurts.
Glad to hear it.
It's nothing serious. Don't worry.
A strong frequency cut the signal soon after you entered the Dark Side. What exactly happened in there?
(Tell her what happened.)
Tacet Discords… they have no business being in the Dark Side. It seems the situation is even more delicate than we thought.
And that student you met—
Sigrika. The Reflections mentioned her name.
What kind of person is she?
She's considered to be a "future Heliodic Six successor." An A-list student, brilliant in deciphering runes, and passionate about every aspect of Academy life.
Never one to hesitate to lend a helping hand. That kindness has earned her many friends.
Including Nivora.
I've seen them together several times. Nivora's Reflection also spoke of her.
We both know who Nivora really is.
You think Sigrika might be the one the Grand Architect was talking about?
She may have just entered the Dark Side by mistake.
Still… <ano=the Grand Architect>Nivora</ano> could have influenced her more than anyone else.
Besides, I'm curious about the riddle she received. She said it's some kind of rune picture.
I've reviewed every registered riddle for the New Solar Celebration. None fit that description.
Then we'll have to speak with her directly.
My thoughts exactly. The New Solar Celebration holds great importance for the Roya. You'll likely find her at the event venue.
Contact me if you need anything, {PlayerName}. I'll assist however I can.

After returning to the real world, you report everything to Lucilla. She identifies the girl you met as Sigrika, a successor to the Heliodic Six. 
To gather more information about the recent changes within the Dark Side, you head to the celebration venue in search of her.
Dr. Luuk and I will speak with the students you saw appear in the Dark Side.
As for Sigrika, I'll leave her to you.
If you need any help with the investigation, come find me. And, {PlayerName}—as a student yourself…
Do take some time to relax and enjoy the celebration.
Huh, {PlayerName}? Hey! Over here!
Long time no see, {PlayerName}! How's it going?
It's great to see you, Lynae.
All good. And you?
Oh, come on! Don't get all formal on me.
I'm great. Just swamped with Academy business lately. Ha, how I miss the days I could just take off whenever I wanted to.
But look at you! Still full of energy, I see.
People seem so out of it these days. Like they've all just crammed for a month of exams in one night.
Anyway, what brings you here? The celebration, I guess?
(Ask Lynae about Sigrika.)
Sigrika? Sure, I know her. We were in the same year. She saved my butt in Royan Cultural History. I never would've passed that exam without her.
Now she's, like, my senior or whatever.
She actually came asking about how we dealt with that Voidworm a while back.
Said she wished she could've been there to help out.
Oh, and she's planning to get your autograph, too!
Since you're looking for her… oh, right! Let's go ask Denia!
She's a friend of Sigrika's and is in charge of the celebration's entrance tickets. She might know where she's at.
Ah, there she is!
The <te href=800031>New Solar Celebration</te> and riddles… Hm, sounds like a blast!
Here I was thinkin' we could use this chance to relax, but that Architect guy from the Fractsidus mentioned it too…
Yes. The Grand Architect treats everything happening here in Lahai-Roi as a game.
Without you, the game in Lahai-Roi would be so boring! So... you get prizes!
At the upcoming New Solar Celebration, among all the lovely students... stands a member of the Fractsidus.
A Fractsidus agent is hiding among the students, biding their time to strike during the celebration.
Maybe that's what President Lucilla wants to discuss.
Makes sense! But what's with the "one from afar" bit?
Probably a riddle.
Let's go see the President and find out what she's planning.
She's not here. Probably off solving riddles again.
That's alright. To be honest, I don't think I can keep doing this anymore. Just... tell her I said thank you.
Made your decision? Well, no one's going to blame you. Not everyone's built like Sigrika.
How does she manage it? No wonder they call her a future Heliodic Six successor...
Your guess is as good as mine. Leaving? Hm, see ya.
Hi, Denia! You know where Sigrika's at?
Here for her help too? You'll have to get in line. She's been running herself ragged with all these riddles lately.
She's after "<te href=800034>Minerva's Treasure</te>" too? But isn't her sorting exam coming up? How's she handling all that?
You'll have to ask her. She's the one who has to live up to the whole "Heliodic Six successor" thing.
Personally? I'd just let it all go. Life would be way easier that way.
Yeah, but then she wouldn't be Sigrika anymore.
Heh. Just wait here, you'll run into her soon enough. She's been in and out of the Dark Side all day. Those portals can open anywhere.
Who knows? Maybe she'll just fall out of the sky.
...Like that.
...Oh, Denia. Did Isla come by?
She did. Said to thank you. Looks like she figured things out on her own, so she doesn't need your help anymore.
Also, you've got visitors.
Upperclassman Lynae! You're back! I'm so glad! And this is… huh?
You're the upperclassman now, Sigrika. {PlayerName} and I are just lowly freshmen.
M-Me? But I...
By the way, this is {PlayerName}, the one I told you about.
Nice to meet you, upperclassman.
Hello. I'm a freshman here.
Wait—you're the {PlayerName}?! I can't believe you're still a freshman…
It's so great to finally meet you! I'm Sigrika. Lynae's told me all about you. I can't believe you're the one who took down the Voidworm!
So, what can I help you with?
Mind if we talk in private?
In private? Uh-oh, sounds serious. You'd better help {Male=him;Female=her} out, Sigrika!
It's nothing serious. I just have a few questions.
Okay then, we should find a quiet spot...
Go see the Divinator. I'll get you tickets.
Good idea! I can pick up the new riddle while we're there. You're the best, Nia!
It's Denia.
Alright, alright. Let's head to the Divinator's place. See you later, Lynae!
Yeah, I'll get back to work! Bye, {PlayerName}. And check your messages when you get a sec.
See ya!
Upon Lynae's suggestion, you meet a girl called Denia. Not long after, you witness Sigrika falling from the sky.
Now off you go. Bye-bye.
...*Yawns* What's up?
About the New Solar Celebration...
About Sigrika...
About you...
Nothing. Thanks.
A celebration of the new sun. For the Roya, it's like... the grand finale of a thousand-year duty.
But duty is just a fancy word for the chains you put on yourself.
Not a fan of responsibilities then?
I think life feels better without the extra weight.
Just ask her whatever you want to know. She'll help you, no doubt.
That's just the kind of predictably boring person she is.
Huh?
Let's get to the venue, Senior.
Interested? This is the Mind Dive Arcade. The games here are so immersive you can easily lose a whole day.
Would you like to try one?
Okay.
Maybe later.
Then let's go see the Divinator first.
Let's go find the Divinator first.
Such a high score… How did you do that?
You'll have to show me your secrets sometime!
I heard the student who solves the most riddles gets a wish granted by the Academy.
What's your wish?
Um... Well... I'd like to get a clear photo of a bird. Yeah!
(She sounds hesitant. Is she trying to hide something?)
No wish greater than that?
Sounds like you just made that up.
This one's good enough for me.
Of course not...
Denia said this year's theme is all about Royan culture. As a Roya myself, it'd be a shame not to take part.
A good friend of mine loved this event too. We used to solve riddles together.
I don't know where she is now, but I want to keep going, for her sake, until she's back.
I think she'd be happy if I did well!
Look, Sigrika just passed your score.
That's a Heliodic Six successor for you. It's only natural.
It would be more of a shock if she couldn't solve them.
Let's go.
Despite earning herself a top position in the event's leaderboard, it's clear that something is bothering Sigrika.
When I met you in the Dark Side yesterday, I didn't realize you were the one in Lynae's stories.
She said you're very determined. You always do whatever it is you set your mind on.
I think that's amazing.
This is it. Denia found this spot. It's quiet, and not many people come here.
See? Perfect for a chat.
Senior, you... wanted to talk about what happened in the Dark Side, right?
(Until I know where she stands, I won't mention the Grand Architect.)
(I'll see what she has to say about the other topics first.)
Denia said you've been busy solving riddles lately.
Yes. It's great fun. I'd like to solve as many as I can before the celebration ends.
Notice anything unusual while you were in there?
Well, everything in there is unusual.
You know how they say the Dark Side reflects people's truest selves?
Once, I saw a black cat that wished to go home. It was a brilliant cat, with black wings to fly.
Another time, I talked for hours with a ghost. I couldn't see her, but I could hear her voice.
Did the black cat find its home?
What did this ghost say?
No, it disappeared in a hurry. Thinking about it now, it was probably someone's delusion.
She said she had to go somewhere far away to do something really important. That she wouldn't see her family for a long time... so she was feeling a little lonely.
But she was so determined... it warmed my heart. I never forgot her.
Such strange, but fascinating experiences. I could spend an entire day in the Dark Side, leaving all my worries behind.
But the disturbances in there lately... You must have heard about them too.
So those things we saw yesterday…
Yes. Especially that rune that lit up out of nowhere.
See? If I just leave it like this, it's just a normal rune.
But, if I invoke its True Name...
…I can call forth the power sleeping within.
What invoked the one we saw before?
I have no idea... I tried to trace the source, but those runes seemed to come from nowhere.
It's worth a closer look. We should go back into the Dark Side.
Then we're going to need this.
Hello? Can you hear me, Didi? Wake up. It's Sigrika. Time to work.
Didi… Scientific Divination Unit. The name "Divinator" is retained due to high student approval ratings.
User login… Sigrika… Switching to Riddle Channel!

Sigrika speaks of the Dark Side's changes, such as the strange runes that keep appearing out of nowhere. While Sigrika is still in the dark about their origin and purpose, they remain a solid lead. 
She suggests using riddles as a way to get back into the Dark Side.
If you would like a riddle, please answer my questions.
A Q&A? Really?
The black of deep space, or the white of the frostlands?
Black.
White.
Any other colors to choose from?
Deep space and the frostlands both represent the unknown... but I like warmer tones. They remind me of the sun.
Solitary study, or collaborative gaming with friends?
Solitary study.
Gaming with friends.
Both.
The gift of miraculous comprehension, or earned recognition from others?
Miraculous comprehension.
Earned recognition from others.
Neither. I do what I want.
You answered so quickly!
You answered so quickly!
I didn't know you could answer like that!
As for me... guess I'll have to work harder then.
Didi... Analyzing data... Presenting results...
Look! Here's the riddle.
It's a picture.
Yes. All of Didi's riddles are pictures. The way into the Dark Side is hidden somewhere in it.
And finding your way out of the Dark Side solves the riddle.
(Inspect the picture.)
Look at where the student is standing. Hmm, I think I recognize this spot...
What's she drawing?
Runes. This one represents the "sun." It's super important to us Roya.
The sun blesses the land with its light. Everyone, from the past to the future, gets to bask in its warmth.
But this sun seems to be feeding the monsters in the shadows, too.
Doesn't that seem wrong?
Now that you mention it... it does. And it reminds me of that rune we saw before.
Then we're on the right track.
Any other clues on the riddle?
Hmm... Oh, here! A line is written on the back—
Through me, seek the "gift"... seek the...
...I've got it!
I think... it's telling us to find this sun.
For <ano=the Roya>us</ano>, the very first rune we ever learn, the most familiar one, is the sun.
To bathe in the blessing of sunlight is its own kind of <ano=gift>inheritance</ano>.
In other words, finding the sun rune is our way into the Dark Side.
That's right. It's just like a game I played when I was little.
The elders of our tribe would hide runes all over Bjartr Woods, each one leading to the next.
All we had to do was follow the trail to find our way out of the forest.
And now, the sun is the first rune in our trail!
To solve this riddle, you're going to need a guide who really knows Royan runes.
(She's right. Otherwise, I'd waste too much time.)
(And if she's connected to the Dark Side's disturbances, it's best I keep an eye on her anyway.)
What's wrong? Don't think I can handle it, Senior? I'm pretty good, you know!
You sure do like to help out, Sigrika.
Then I'll be counting on you.
Let's solve this riddle together.
Great! You can trust me, {PlayerName}! Let's go find the answer!

You receive a riddle from the Divinator hinting at a deeper transformation within the Dark Side—it is secretly spawning monsters. 
Sigrika realizes that the riddle-solving process resembles a game from her childhood and volunteers to guide you through it.
Mm, I see. I'll count on you, then, Dr. Luuk.
Ah, {PlayerName}'s here. I'll brief {Male=him;Female=her} on the situation.
President Lucilla! Whaddya need us for? And what did you mean by "the one from afar who touches 'truth'"?
Why don't you take a guess? It means exactly what it says.
...The one from afar is me.
And the "truth" that reveals the door of dreams is...?
The "truth" has waited for you here for a very long time.
That pillow is truth? Seriously?
What's that for? A nice nap?
You need a good pillow for a good sleep. When it comes to "dreams," that is the absolute truth.
Oh, quite. When it comes to "dreams," that is the absolute truth.
A simple riddle, isn't it?
Okay... but what's that got to do with us? You didn't call us here to solve a riddle, right?
You're here because I'd like you to investigate the "<te href=800032>Academy's Dark Side</te>." We believe the Grand Architect's next move is tied to it.
The Academy's Dark Side?
Think of it as the "Academy's reflection." It's a special Sonoro Sphere formed from the collective emotions of the student body.
It's an inseparable part of this place, a mirror into the thoughts of every student here... a reflection of their "youth."
"Youth"... a fascinating concept. So full of ideals and passion, but also anxiety and sorrow.
The Dark Side absorbs it all, reflecting their truest selves back at them. And that's not all.
Imagine the Dark Side as a vortex. Negative emotions like anxiety and sorrow form its eye.
They swell and collide, seeping into the students' minds. Over time, they come to affect the Academy itself.
Exactly. It's been troubling the Academy for some time.
Many of our students were hailed as "geniuses" back home, only to discover they're just one of many here. They've given their all, yet find the peak they once stood upon is now out of reach.
The Dark Side forces these young minds to face that "truth."
A harsh reality that can be difficult to accept.
In the end, the Research Institute found a way to confine the Dark Side within "riddles," turning it into a harmless game that students look forward to each year.
Solve a riddle, face your problems, and process the emotions festering in the Dark Side. That was the original purpose.
But recently, something's changed. Dr. Luuk has reported a sharp decline in students' mental states, as if they've been influenced somehow.
At the same time, I've detected a frequency in the Dark Side that doesn't belong to the Academy. It can't be traced or analyzed.

We need more information, but we're stretched thin. Between hunting for the Fractsidus agent, the celebration approaching, and dealing with the students' deteriorating mental health, it's not looking good.
The Grand Architect intends to create chaos in the Academy to conceal their true objective.
Looks like we'll have to investigate the Academy's Dark Side ourselves.
So this pillow… is the key to getting in?
Hehe, I see! This is our starting line!
Yes. Head in whenever you're ready.
I'll stay outside and keep in touch.

Lucilla detects a strange frequency not belonging to the Academy in its "Dark Side." Coincidentally, many students have been reporting mental disturbances. Suspecting a connection to the "game" the Grand Architect mentioned, you agree to Lucilla's request and enter the Dark Side to investigate.
(I should send this riddle to the President as well. She might find something we missed.)
(Send the riddle.)
Lucilla, Sigrika and I received this riddle from the Divinator at the celebration venue.
I've attached the photo and the coordinates.
I'm concerned other students might be getting similar riddles.
Very well. We'll trace it.
Focus on your investigation and be careful.
Found it! It should be here.
There's nothing here.
Do you have a camera?
Let me enchant it with a rune...
There! Now you should be able to see it too.
Here, try taking a picture.
There it is.
A concealed rune won't escape these eyes.
Now, I'll illuminate its True Name.
If we follow its guidance, we'll find the answer.
Look! A trail of runes to follow.
You know, when I played that rune-finding game in Bjartr Woods, I was always the first one out.
Everyone said I had a gift, that I'd be the first again, and again, and again.
They all believed in me, so... I had to work even harder to live up to it.
I spent so, so much time studying those runes.
Before I realized it, everyone had moved on to more important things… and the game had already ended.
See, all the runes lead to this place.
It has to be an entrance into the Dark Side.
Remember you asked what I was doing in the Dark Side yesterday?
I was there for the riddles. But what about you?
I'm looking into something important. I'm sorry, but I can't share the details with you yet.
Oh that's okay. Just tell me whenever you're ready.
Time to illuminate this rune. On three… two…
One...
Say cheese!
All clear. No lasting harm. If the runes didn't answer, it's likely because your heart wasn't quite in the right place to call them.
Do you still remember what I told you last time, Sigrika?
You said I should have more faith in myself.
And not to push yourself too hard. Here, have a candy.
That song we heard… it felt nostalgic.
You liked it?
It was beautiful.
It was effective.
Fleet Snowfluff's songs always do bring a little warmth to the soul.
Aemeath...
When we tried to enter the Dark Side to help you, we found our frequencies denied. That thing hijacked it. Only students were allowed in.
So we had to get creative. Luckily, it can't control the emotions already inside.
Every emotion leaves a mark. The fan meeting you held not long ago left the students with plenty of happy memories. That song helped guide you right through that thing's blockade.
It's no ordinary Tacet Discord. What exactly is that thing?
I want to know what I saw in there.
Please trust me, Professor… and you too, Senior. I really want to help.
Let's share what we know so far.
We need the full picture to act.
What we can confirm is that the Tacet Discord was sent by the Fractsidus.
The Fractsidus?
Their Grand Architect tipped us off about their plans in Lahai-Roi, that he will play a game with us.
It seems the "game" she referred to is this riddle event our students are so fond of.
We've already tracked the participants. The effects of these riddles are already spilling into their real lives.
Some abandoned their research projects. Others are lashing out at classmates... and a few have lost control entirely.
These students have all become pawns in the Fractsidus's little show.
You're one of them, Sigrika.
Me?
Those riddles and Tacet Discords we encountered in the Dark Side were clearly targeting you.
I thought you might've been linked to the recent disturbances, but now I see you're just another victim.
What kind of game would do something like this?! We have to stop it. I can help you solve these strange riddles.
Professor, Senior, please let me...
You need to rest, Sigrika.
Why? Because I'm not good enough?
Because it's dangerous.
I see how determined you are. But I won't risk my students' safety.
(Comfort her.)
(Support Lucilla.)
Just take it easy for now, Sigrika.
The President is right. The Dark Side is too volatile right now. Just take it easy.
Senior...
...I see.

Back outside the Dark Side, you piece together what just happened. 
You realize those Tacet Discords you ran into are the work of the Fractsidus trying to drag students into their twisted game. 
Lucilla urges Sigrika not to endanger herself further, but the girl has her own ideas about what she wants to do.
Hello, {PlayerName}. You free?
Wanna try some Rainbow Bean Pop Rocks?
What's that…?
You'll know once you're here!
I'll be waiting at the celebration venue. Please do come find me!
What's that…?
Senior, you're here!
I bought this for you. As a thank-you for your help lately.
Here, it's all yours!
This is...
...Rainbow Bean Pop Rocks?
That's right! This one is made specially for the New Solar Celebration. I had to wait in line forever to get it.
You never know what funny effect you're going to get! It's different each time. But it's supposed to help with relaxing.
Go on, give it a try!
(Take one boldly.)
(Take one hesitantly.)
(Return it to Sigrika.)
Are you sure? Oh, look—a big rabbit over there!
Rabbit—?
[Sigrika pops a candy into Rover's mouth.]
...It actually tastes pretty good. Wait—is that my voice?!
Can this really be?!
Let me try one too...
This one… tastes kind of weird—hic! Oh no—no, I wasn't—hic! Excuse me!
I have lungs, not gills—hic!
I—I can't—hic—make it stop!
Abby.
Huh? Think you can just summon—Hey, what did ya just give me—?!
Chuuu?!
Hic—you sound like a Soliskin now!
This is so much fun! Maybe we should share them with others too!
Hic... Don't worry, it won't last long and—hic!
I'm okay. But you'd better take it easy...
...If you're looking for a particular stall, find Lynae. If you're hurt, call N.A.N.A. If you lost something, bother that one running around in computers. Just don't bother me, kay?
But I'm here for you, Nia—hic!
Give me a break, Siggy. Can't a girl just enjoy the fresh air on a beautiful, class-free morning?
Oh, never mind. Your... excessive cheerfulness just ruined the vibe. Surprised you bounced back so fast. What do you want?
We want to—hic!
...Do you want some water?
Sigrika wants you to try this.
Why does your voice sound like that?
Chuuu!
Just try it, Denia! Please?
…I refuse. The last time I tried your candy, I was shooting rainbow beams out of my eyes and mouth for four minutes and thirty-five seconds.
That was a research sample! This is real! Just trust me and try—hic!
Case in point. Can someone just solve the riddle of who made these strange candies in the first place?
If you must find someone to try it, look for Lynae. She'd probably love this stuff. Here, take your tickets.
Now shoo. See you.
Hi, {PlayerName}! Hi, Sigrika!
Hello, Lynae—Hic!
Why are you hiccuping, Sigrika?
We just had some Rainbow Bean Pop Rocks. Want to try?
No way! Your voice is totally different! Gimme one, I gotta try this!
Lynae takes the candy under your watchful eyes.
Oh, that's "awful"!
Wait, that "is" what I meant...? Everything is "the same as" what I want to say... Huh?
It's making you say the opposite of what you're trying to say...
Hahaha! This is "not" interesting, how "boring" is this!
Oh, by the way, how's that thing from the other day going?
Still working on it.
I'll make sure it gets handled.
Aha, so you're taking a break, huh?
In that case, you need to take a "bad" rest! Here, I "don't think you should " try this game I co-developed. We "shouldn't" play ten rounds!
Ten rounds it is!
I'm good. You two play it.
Raiding time!!!
Alright. It's gaming time, Sigrika!
I want to try it! What do you say?
You watch them play. Lynae's hands fly across the screen with ease, while Sigrika scrambles to keep up. It's a welcome moment of peace.
Phew, I always "hate" playing games.
Ahem, I mean... Never mind. You know what I was trying to say.
You can't talk normally, Lynae.
But don't worry, the effect won't last long. I've already stopped hiccuping.
"It's not okay," talking like this is actually really "boring." I'm having a "terrible" time!
…I'll wait for it to wear off before I say anything else.
By the way, do you still have any candies left?
Of course! I bought plenty. Here.
Thanks! This candy is "the worst." I want some other people to try it too, you know, share the "frowns"!
Have a "bad" day, you two!
We will. Enjoy your day!
Have a "bad" day... Have a good day...
Different words, but you can tell what she means. I guess it's because Lynae always speaks from the heart.
That's really nice.
So? Did you have a good day?
Hm? Your voice is back to normal.
Well, I guess I had quite a "terrible" rest.
Everyone's enjoying the celebration.
When I close my eyes, I can hear all kinds of voices around me.
Vendors calling out new kinds of Pop Rocks, students cheering about new records...
Some are singing, others are just laughing... It's so nice.
But... besides all that, I still hear other voices.
The ones from the Dark Side, when I let everyone down... they made me feel so lost.
After we got out, you told me to walk away. For my own safety.
But when I was alone, I remembered all the encouraging things people have always said to me.
"You're going to do great things." "You can solve any riddle." "You can do it." "You can prove yourself"...
Their words shone like molten gold. So radiant, and so... warm.
I don't want to be the one to drop them.
So many voices... When you hear so many voices in your head, Senior, what do you do?
I listen to what my own heart is saying.
I try to live up to their expectations.
Hehe, I like that. The voice of my heart... I've never thought of that before.
Right... Living up to their expectations!
But sometimes, you don't need to overthink what you do.
Don't overthink what to do… So you mean just—
Just do it.
We have an emergency, {PlayerName}. Multiple students are trapped in the Dark Side.
I've already sent the coordinates.
The Fractsidus are making their move. The situation is serious.
I'm coming with you.
Alright. Let's go.

Amid the lively and relaxed atmosphere of the celebration, Sigrika opens up about what's been on her mind. 
Before you can talk long, Lucilla cuts in with an urgent message.
Good, you're here, {PlayerName}. And... Sigrika.
Professor Lucilla...
I know you want to prove yourself. But in your current state, I'm afraid I can't authorize it.
You must understand how dangerous this mission is. Our enemies hide in the dark, and we still don't know that Tacet Discord's weakness.
No, Professor. Please let me join.
I know you're worried, but I know the Dark Side better than anyone. How can I stand by when my classmates are trapped in there?
I can help you decipher the runes. I can be useful.
This isn't about proving myself anymore. It's about doing what's right.
She's made up her mind.
She can contribute to the mission.
These are the students whose signals vanished while in the Dark Side.
Chisa...? She's trapped in there too?
And also Isla?! Why would she...
They were all in the dormitory hall. Someone must have activated a riddle, and the Dark Side pulled everyone in.
Mornye is already on it. I have her searching for the students' locations.
And this is what I found.
Another runic riddle.
"Receive the sunlight's blessing"... What could it mean?
It's referring to our rite of passage.
When a Roya is deemed ready to bear the tribe's expectations, we go to the Reactor Drive to be Imprinted.
The Drive leaves markings on our bodies. It is a blessing for the next stage of our lives.
See these? They are the sunlight's Imprints.
So... you're already considered an adult?
Yes! Three years ahead of schedule. Impressive, right?
The Roya value the gifted and the strong. Adulthood is defined by passing the Imprinting Trial. It's a rite of great importance.
Yes, it's true. I still remember how thrilled I was the night before my Imprinting.
"I will be an adult." "Does that mean everything will finally come easier?"
I wonder if the girl in this picture thought the same. But it doesn't look like she's receiving a blessing... more like she's being chained by it.
(Flip it over.)
(Just as I thought, there's something written on the back.)
"Don't think, don't look. To escape from emotion, one must break the chains of expectation."
"The chains of expectation"... That fits the picture. What if this rune is another ticket into the Dark Side?
Sigrika, can you decipher it and find the entrance?
No problem.
Then we're going in and bringing everyone back.
Wait.
Professor Lucilla, I—
I won't stand in your way if your mind is made up. But you will take this.
...Her record.
Consider it a "backup." If the situation turns, play it. The diva who travels through time will come bearing azure echoes.
I see you mean that literally.
Indeed, I do.
Don't let the shadows inside get the better of you.
Come back safe. Both of you.
What is the person who made this riddle trying to say?
Check the back.

Several students' signals have vanished within the Dark Side, and at each disappearance site, Lucilla discovered a riddle. 
Following its lead, you must re-enter the Dark Side to rescue them.
Huh? The trail of runes is leading straight to my dormitory...?
I haven't tidied up... Er, please don't laugh when you go in, okay?

Another day with a full schedule. Still can't wrap my head around Energy Grid Planning. Need to review that again.

Tomorrow's going to be tough: Topology, Dynamics, and Material Sciences. Should probably prep tonight. Oh, and that presentation before Materials class!

Then there's an exam the day after tomorrow… better start revising soon.
…
Why is school so hard?

It's okay, Sigrika. You've got this! Everyone says you're going to be one of the successors to the Heliodic Six. These classes shouldn't be a problem for you!
…

Then why does it feel SO HARD?!

Nivora told me I could solve any riddle. So I'll definitely figure out the ones in the Dark Side.

I can do this. I have to do this.
I'm Sigrika.
…

But what if I fail?
What if I lose control of the runes, like last time? What if I just make things worse for {PlayerName}?
…

Stop thinking like this, Sigrika.

A photo of Sigrika, Denia, and Nivora. 
"Best Friends Forever!"
Hiding the way in a drawer? I never imagined this would be a good place to store all those emotions...
Guess we'll have to squeeze our way in!
You give Abby a gentle pat and step into the laughter and cheers of the Celebration.
It's okay not to know.
Just stay here.
A break from thinking will feel so good.
These people are all talking to the same voice.
Is it influencing them?
Maybe... the signal...
President Lucilla?
...Hello? Can you hear—
Find the clock... leave... wait for you outside...
Signal's been cut off.
Be careful, {PlayerName}. Something smells off here.
I think it's coming from over there.
Let's go take a look.
A rabbit.
Looks like it's waiting for us.
You're finally here, Sigrika! Took you long enough!
You're always studying those runes and never have time for us!
(Sigrika... I've heard that name before.)
Look at this, Sigrika! You have a gift. I'm sure you know what this means!
I believe in you, my dear girl.
You will become an excellent Solsworn.
This is... too much for me... I can't do it...
Classes all day today, tomorrow, and the day after! Ugh!
I'm so done...
What exactly am I supposed to...
Go away!
Boo! Boring!
Blah, blah, blah!
Sorry, I think I'll just...
I'll give up...
Let's call it here.
Hurry up, or you'll be late for the experiment!
Hurry...
(Is that Nivora?)
Why does this feel like a dead end... The records...
That was amazing! I knew you could solve it!
No matter what kind of riddle it is, you'll always find the answer!
You really are a future Heliodic Six successor!
These frequencies smell different now... Stay alert, {PlayerName}!
What's the point of working so hard...?
Why not just tell them you can't do it and quit, hm?
I hereby illuminate this rune—
Begone... at once!
Are you okay? Did I help you?
I'm fine. Thank you.
A great help, actually.
Oh, I know you! You're that freshman who was muttering to {Male=himself;Female=herself} in class.
How is it that you're here?
Me? I'm here to solve the riddles, of course!
The picture in my riddle led me here.
But everything's become a bit of a mess. Strange runes were scrawled all over the place. But I've almost figured it all out. The exit is just—
...Behind you.
These runes again...
Come on. We're getting out of here.

While exploring, you encounter abnormal Reflections and Tacet Discords. During a confrontation with one of the Tacet Discords, a girl appears and helps you. She insists on guiding you out of the Dark Side.
We need to find an alarm clock to leave.
I know. Just follow these runes. Trust me.
What kind of riddle did you get?
A picture made of runes. What was yours?
Oh, this way!
I think this is the clock!
So this is the Academy's Dark Side...
That's right. Do you see them? The floating astronaut helmets, along with the desks and chairs?
They are the students' delusions. They took root in the Academy and were shaped by their emotions.
Chapters in their "youth," you might say.
Let's move on. You need to find the "alarm clock."
Alarm clock?
A pillow is an invitation to sleep, just as an alarm clock is a call to wake up. It is your key to leaving this place.
When the Institute bound the Dark Side, they took great care to ensure the students' safety. Every riddle has an answer.
And if the key proves elusive, there are always a few backup measures.
So, breathe easy.
There's someone ahead.
Likely a Reflection made by the Dark Side.
Hm, that particular student, though...
The data's wrong no matter how you look at it! Wrong! Wrong! WRONG!
What else can I do...? I've tried everything...
Excuse me?
You mean... I should just give up? But...
She's not talking to you, {PlayerName}.
Right. It's not like anyone on the team would miss me anyway...
She's no longer a student here.
I remember her. She once proposed a few research hypotheses. Brilliant, just headed in the wrong direction.
But she never could get past that.
The Dark Side absorbed her pain. And here it remains.
I'm so… so tired… I don't know what to do anymore.
Then why not stay? You've thought about it, haven't you?
Just give all your worries to me. Relax. Let me help you.
This voice is...
Track it down.
I don't know...
The things you said… I never thought of it that way before...
You can do it that way? That's amazing.
Sigrika.
Copy!
This way.
Okay!
Why are the runes interrupted... Am I too slow?
I need to clear the interruptions. But how should I do it...
I can help. It seems we can smash down from mid-air to clear the interruptions.
We need to correct them.
That's it!
Are you alright? Just keep trying to correct it.
Wait, I see it now! The gravity is reversed here. That grapple point will take us up.
By the Flow of their True Name... I will make this path!
Are you sure you can?
You're pushing yourself too hard, my dear…
Do not waste your gifts.
…Ugh!
Do you really believe in yourself? Do others trust you?
Stay here, just stay here... stay with these feelings...
Everything will feel so simple. So simple. So simple...
What was that noise...
I heard it too. Not sure who was speaking, but… it made me feel uncomfortable.
Let's head that way and see what's going on.
Follow me. I've been here a few times.
...There's a black hole on the blackboard.
Oh, that one. It's proof of all the tired students dozing off during early 8 o'clock lectures.
Another 8 a.m. lecture… Why do I have them every single day?! If only they could disappear forever!
This blackboard is just like a black hole... Sucking my brain into it...
It's a rune.
I just illuminated its True Name.
We can teleport from here now.
Is that her? One of the successors of the Heliodic Six?
She looks so young. Are the Roya really just going to entrust their future to her…?
Just wait and see how she handles those questions.
These are Reflections created by the Dark Side.
The deepest truths within them… So that's how they see me.
Come, my sweet Sigrika. Solve this riddle.
Mother?
Use the ability that brings you such pride. Waiting is the path to your "gift."
I never expected to see my mother's <te href=800033>Reflection</te> here.
She's always been proud of me… I won't let her down.
Let's take a look at this riddle together then.
Yes! We should be able to teleport from here. But… the Flow of its True Name is reversed.
I know… let's try this! This rune can correct it for you!
That rune looks similar to the one I saw yesterday.
I'll go decipher it.
The riddle is solved! I knew you two could do it!
No puzzle can stand against the two of you!
Go for it! I'm rooting for you!
Forward you must go, my child.
Solve all the riddles, and an excellent Solsworn you will become.
…I understand.
No matter what the next riddle is, I will solve it.
I solved it! But why…
Too slow, Sigrika… You should have done better.
Are you feeling unwell?
I…
We've already solved this riddle. Nothing for you here, Tacet Discords.
How could it be…
What went wrong?
Are you feeling unwell?
So this is what Mother thought when I wasn't good enough.
I don't want to disappoint her...
You are good enough.
We've solved the riddles. We already know our next move now.
Time's ticking, Sigrika—
(These Reflections keep pressuring her.)
(Or maybe… this whole place is designed to break her.)
Sorry, I—
Don't worry, Sigrika. You're not in this alone.
Riddle solved!
Still too slow, Sigrika. You spend all your time deciphering runes. When do you ever have time for us...
Or is it that you don't even have a wish? Is that why you can't focus?
I'm sorry… I'll try to be faster…
Breathe, Sigrika.
I am breathing. I just... if I can adjust the resonance equations, I can speed this up. Then the Tacet Discords won't show up, and I—
Even if they do, we'll deal with them together.
But Mother and Nivora…  I'm letting them down.
If that's what they really think of me, then I have to—
It doesn't matter. They're only Reflections born of the Dark Side.
They've been targeting you from the start. Don't give them what they want.
See? The path is still forming, even with the Tacet Discords here.
That proves we're on the right track.
With your gifts, you should be much faster than this...
Sigrika... You can do it...
Faster this time... I have to be faster...
Yes, Sigrika, you can do this. These runes won't beat you...
Hmph... It's already the final riddle. Your last chance to prove yourself...
Tell me, what does a gift, or an endowment, mean to the Roya? I want to hear your answer.
You can do this, my sweet child...
Hm... the answer is...
I believe in you.
Just follow your heart.
To us Roya, the sun is an endowment. For me, the sun is also the first rune I ever learned.
So it has to be the key to unlocking any gift.
It's wrong? But why...?
A child who fails does not get to leave.
That voice again.
No... my runes! They're not responding?!
See? My little pet performed wonderfully, don't you think?
So, {PlayerName}, what will you do with all these emotions?
That's it. I believe in my choice.
The answer... is the sun.
Sorrow, disappointment, self-doubt... Why do you still keep going...
Our attacks are useless. It's feeding on the surrounding energy!
There you are.
I'll be watching. I'm going to cherish every second... of the time you've bought me.
Are you alright, Senior?
Did you get hurt?
You've never dared to imagine yourself failing. You fear their comfort… just as much as their blame.
Is it because you lack the confidence to face falling short? Or because you're arrogant enough to believe you ever could?
You... how do you...?
How can I know this?
Because I am you.
Haven't you wondered? Why Isla saw you in the Dark Side? Why all those riddles mirrored your life?
Every change in the Dark Side, every strange whisper you heard, every Tacet Discord you faced…
They all came from you, Sigrika.
...And you, you'll always end up back here.
In the nightmare, Sigrika comes to believe that all the Dark Side's changes are being caused by herself.
(Seems I can smash on the drum to bounce up...)
(Maybe I can step on the notes to get up there.)
I can help. It seems we can crush the interruptions from above.
Is that the girl...?
Too slow, Sigrika.
You can be faster.
Why aren't you being serious?
Come on! Faster!
If you can't do...
Need your help for these as well...
Can you help me check this?
Maybe you can do it...
I'm counting on you!
Still have these ones, too...
You got it wrong. Again.
Too slow. That can't be right.
That's all you've got?
Aaahh... Hurry...
Stay... Stay here...
Stay here...
Stay with us...
You don't need it...
Can you...? Mnh...
This Tacet Discord is just like the one we saw before!
STAY... STAY...
STAY HERE... STAY...
Looks like I can make sound waves by smashing into the ground.
This microphone might come in handy.
You're here!
Thank goodness you're safe.
Everything suddenly looked so huge... Well, guess I just shrank.
It definitely helped us see the world from a better angle.
Did the rabbit… get bigger?
No. I've just gotten smaller.
Oh, look! Rabbits!
There are two of them!
We'll split up and follow them.
(This place… it looks almost the same as last time.)
Senior, Professor Mornye is calling.
Hello, Mentor? Can you hear me?
Loud and clear.
…Good. I've regained partial control over the Dark Side. Several areas are stabilizing.
I've found a few of the students. I'm sending their coordinates to your Terminal now.
We're counting on you to bring them back, {PlayerName}.
We found you, Chisa!
...Ah, hello.
One moment. I'm analyzing this piano. If we integrate an electric grill into it and map each key to a control knob...
...Apologies. I was simply occupying my time after getting lost.
How did you end up here, Chisa?
The reason is unclear. I was en route to submit a project to the Student Council, but a debate was occurring, so I was waiting nearby.
Then I suddenly felt dizzy. When I opened my eyes, I was here.
Later, I followed a rabbit for a while, and that's when I ran into some trouble.
You mean the Reflections?
You mean the Tacet Discords?
